meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Pricing to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to implement SaaS pricing to overcome a lack of portfolio. An intermediate-level checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code for AI builders.
content.answer_block: To overcome a lack of portfolio, build a functional SaaS pricing model that demonstrates business logic. By integrating Stripe API with intelligent rate-limiting via Antigravity and Claude Code, you showcase end-to-end engineering skills. This transforms a simple student project into a professional-grade product that recruiters and clients immediately respect.
content.intro:
The blank screen of a portfolio is the silent killer of early-career momentum. You have the technical skills, but the lack of a 'real-world' project keeps you invisible to hiring managers and potential clients. The difference between a hobbyist and a professional is the ability to bridge code with commerce.

Today, we stop building 'to-do lists' and start building infrastructure. By implementing a sophisticated SaaS pricing engine, you prove you understand user lifecycle, state management, and secure financial integration—the trifecta of production-grade software.

steps.title: Define Tiered Architecture
steps.description: Establish the logic for Free, Pro, and Enterprise tiers. This demonstrates your ability to model complex business requirements into database schemas.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a database schema for a SaaS pricing table including fields for tier name, price, stripe_plan_id, and feature_flag_json.

steps.title: Integrate Stripe Webhooks
steps.description: Securely handle subscription events. A robust webhook listener proves you can handle asynchronous data and external API security.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a secure Express.js webhook handler for Stripe events including 'customer.subscription.created' and 'invoice.payment_failed'.

steps.title: Implement Feature Gating
steps.description: Connect your pricing tiers to actual application features. This is the 'aha' moment where your code starts enforcing business rules.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a middleware function that checks the user's subscription tier against requested API endpoints and returns 403 if restricted.

steps.title: Build the Pricing UI
steps.description: Create a high-converting, responsive pricing table. UI/UX is the final layer that separates a prototype from a product.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a React pricing component with toggleable monthly/yearly billing and 'recommended' badge styling using Tailwind CSS.

steps.title: Simulate Production Load
steps.description: Use load testing to ensure your pricing logic holds up under stress, proving you think about scalability.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a load testing script to simulate 50 concurrent users upgrading their subscription tiers simultaneously.

pro_tips: Always store prices in cents to avoid floating-point math errors common in financial applications.
Implement a 'grace period' logic in your database to prevent immediate service disruption on failed payments.
Use environment variables for all Stripe keys; never hardcode secrets, even in development.
Add an 'Enterprise' contact button that triggers a webhook to your CRM to show you understand sales funnels.
Document your API response times for payment processing in your README to highlight performance optimization.
content.next_steps: Deploy your application to Vercel or Railway, document the architecture in your GitHub README, and share your live demo on LinkedIn to showcase your new production-ready SaaS feature.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to turn a 'project' into a 'product' that employers can't ignore. Stop waiting for permission to build. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
